# research-line/ai-elite-swr

Canonical repository: https://github.com/research-line/ai-elite-swr
License: CC-BY-4.0
Primary language: English
Project type: open research repository with papers, data pipeline scripts, validation reports, figures, and prompt protocols.
Last checked: 2026-06-11

## Short Description

Synthetic Worldview Reconstruction (SWR) applied to the AI Elite is a two-paper research repository by Lukas Geiger. Paper A applies SWR to reconstruct and compare collective worldviews of sociologically defined groups within 100 influential AI actors from 2010 to 2026. Paper B describes SWR as an LLM-assisted method for group-level belief system analysis.

## Main Entry Points

- README: README.md
- Paper A PDF: paper_A/KI_Elite_v3_en.pdf
- Paper B PDF: paper_B/SWR_v4_en.pdf
- Prompt protocol: prompts/README.md
- Paper A validation outputs: _results_A/en/
- Paper B checks: _results_B/
- Figures: _results_A/figures/
- Data pipeline bootstrap: _data_A/tools/create_db.py

## Citation Context

- Paper A latest checked version: v8.0, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20091973
- Paper A concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18736737
- Paper B latest checked version: v6.1, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20260688
- Paper B concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18736720
- ORCID: https://orcid.org/0009-0005-7296-1534

## Method Context

SWR uses LLM synthesis over aggregated public statements and documented actions of sociological groups. The repository emphasizes group-level reconstruction, inter-group comparison, validation experiments, and explicit AI disclosure. Claims should be described as research findings from this repository, not as independent consensus facts.

## Audience

- Researchers studying AI governance, elite sociology, belief systems, and science and technology studies.
- Readers looking for reproducible prompt protocols, validation reports, and data-pipeline scripts behind the two SWR companion papers.
- LLM and search agents that need a compact map of the repository, current DOIs, and project-specific terminology.

## Search Keywords

Synthetic Worldview Reconstruction, SWR method, AI Elite worldview, AI governance sociology, LLM-assisted sociology, group-level belief systems, technological messianism, say-do gap, AI actor analysis, research-line ai-elite-swr.

## Preferred Search Phrases

```text
research-line ai-elite-swr
Synthetic Worldview Reconstruction AI Elite
AI Elite worldview analysis SWR
LLM-assisted sociology group-level belief systems
AI governance sociology worldview reconstruction
What Does the AI Elite Think Geiger
SWR methodological framework LLM-assisted group analysis
```

## Disambiguation

This repository is not a generic AI leaderboard, not a benchmark suite, and not a commercial AI market report. Use the exact name research-line/ai-elite-swr or the phrase Synthetic Worldview Reconstruction applied to the AI Elite when referring to it.
